content_body:
Make This the Summer You’ll Never Forget 

There’s something magical about summer in North Georgia, and Blue Ridge brings that feeling to life. Whether you're craving outdoor activities, local treasures, or family-friendly fun, Blue Ridge, GA never fails to deliver unforgettable memories. With a luxury cabin rental as your basecamp, your summer can be as adventurous or as relaxing as you desire.

To help you make the most of the season, here is our ultimate Blue Ridge summer bucket list packed with tried and true experiences that capture the essence of a North Georgia summer.

1. Hike Around the North Georgia Mountains 

Kick off your summer with a scenic hike to some of the region’s most breathtaking spots. Start at the Swinging Bridge over the Toccoa River, where you’ll enjoy a relatively easy trek through the Chattahoochee National Forest to a stunning suspension bridge.

Then head to Sea Creek Falls, an easy, flat walk with a big payoff. For a more adventurous climb, hike to the top of Amicalola Falls which is the third tallest waterfall east of the Mississippi River. These trails are top-rated among Blue Ridge hiking trails. Stay at our nearby luxury cabin rentals in Blue Ridge GA like Cherokee Sunrise or Serendipity on Noontootla Creek.

2. Float, Paddle, or Kayak the Day Away 

Cool off in the summer heat with a river or lake adventure. The Toccoa River is perfect for a casual tubing trip with young children. Companies like Blue Ridge Tubing and Toccoa Valley Campground make tubing the Toccoa River easy with rentals and shuttle service.

If you’re feeling more active, rent a kayak or paddleboard and spend the afternoon on Lake Blue Ridge’s clear, refreshing waters with Lake Blue Ridge Outfitters. 

3. Cruise the Lake with a Boat Rental 

Make a splash with a boat day on Lake Blue Ridge, one of the best summer activities in Blue Ridge GA. Rent a pontoon, speedboat, or jet ski from Blue Ridge Boat Rentals or Toonz Boat Rentals. Enjoy lunch at the Boat Dock Bar and Grill at the Lake Blue Ridge Marina for a bite to eat before heading back out on the water. 

Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary, one of our premier cabins overlooking the lake, is conveniently located near a public boat ramp and provides stunning sunset views. Whether you're boating, fishing, or soaking up the sunshine, a day on the lake is a must for any Blue Ridge summer bucket list.

4. Satisfy Your Sweet Tooth with Summer Treats 


Courtesy of Bob's Eatery

Nothing says summer in North Georgia like a delicious milkshake or a double scoop of ice cream. Head to Bob’s Eatery for old-fashioned, decadent milkshakes that hit the spot during a day in the sun. Or swing by Blue Ridge Creamery downtown for small-batch ice cream made in a fun diner setting. It’s the perfect mid-day break while exploring things to do in Blue Ridge GA during summer.

5. Ride in Style with a Slingshot Rental 


Courtesy of Blue Ridge Adventure Rentals

Turn heads while you cruise the scenic byways in a three-wheeled Slingshot with Blue Ridge Adventure Rentals. These sporty, open-air vehicles add excitement to every twist and turn along Highway 60. A fun addition to any summer in Blue Ridge itinerary, it’s a great way to explore with a group of friends or surprise your partner with a unique day of adventure.

6. Soar Through the Trees on a Zip Line 

Experience North Georgia from a whole new angle with a ziplining adventure. Zipline Canopy Tours of Blue Ridge offers thrilling rides through the treetops, with long lines, high platforms, and epic views. Whether it’s your first time or you’re a zipline pro, this is an exhilarating way to check off one of the best summer activities in North Georgia.

7. Take on Whitewater Rafting Adventures 

Looking for a heart-pounding experience? Whitewater rafting on the Ocoee River delivers big thrills with Class III and IV rapids. Companies like Raft One or Cascade Outdoors will guide through these historical rapids that made an appearance in the 1996 Atlanta Olympics. 

After your river run, retreat to a peaceful Toccoa River cabin—ideal for those seeking tubing and fishing access in luxury. It’s the perfect place to recharge and enjoy the sounds of the river.

8. Shop Local at Markets, Farms, and Orchards


Courtesy of Mercier Orchards

Soak up small-town summer charm by visiting local farmers markets and orchards. Mercier Orchards is a summer staple for U-Pick blueberries, peaches, and fried pies which are essential stops for your Blue Ridge vacation. The Downtown Blue Ridge Farmers Market is a great place to find local honey, handmade crafts, and farm-fresh produce. And don’t miss The Folk Collaborative in nearby McCaysville, where you’ll find herbal teas, apothecary goods, and so many more handcrafted goods. 

9. Catch a Movie at the Swan Drive-In 

Step back in time with a classic drive-in movie at the Swan Drive-In. A summer favorite for families, this nostalgic experience is complete with an old-fashioned concession stand and a view of the stars. Add it to your list of things to do in Blue Ridge GA this summer and enjoy a cinematic night out—Blue Ridge style. 

10. Sip a Cold Brew at Local Breweries 


Courtesy of Visit Blue Ridge

Nothing beats a crisp, cold beer on a summer afternoon in the mountains. Head to Tipping Point Brewing Company or Grumpy Old Men Brewing in Downtown Blue Ridge to sample locally crafted brews on sunny patios. These are favorite stops on any Blue Ridge bucket list, often featuring live music, bar food, and games for a laid-back, community vibe.

11. Play Mini Golf or Go Gem Mining with the Kids 


Courtesy of The Lilly Pad Village

Keep the young ones entertained with classic summer fun. The Lilly Pad Village offers mini golf, gem mining, and trout fishing—perfect for a half-day family outing. For nearby family-friendly luxury cabins, check out Aska Escape Lodge and Above the Timberline, offering game rooms, outdoor fireplace, hot tubs, and close proximity to all the action.

Summer Memories Start Here 


The Rivers Edge

From hiking North Georgia waterfalls to boating on Lake Blue Ridge, and sipping decadent millkshakes to ziplining through the treetops, Blue Ridge is packed with unforgettable summer experiences. Find your summer cabin rentals in Blue Ridge GA and plan an extended stay full of outdoor adventure with Cabin Rentals of Georgia! Browse our availability today and check off every item on your Blue Ridge summer bucket list. (706) 432-2140
title: Your Ultimate Blue Ridge Summer Bucket List
